about.title:
Engineer.
Builder.
Founder.
paragraphs: I'm a Mechanical Engineering student at UT Austin (graduating December 2026) with a Minor in Entrepreneurship, building hardware systems where tight tolerances and high stakes are the standard.

My work spans defense manufacturing at Northrop Grumman — developing Python ML pipelines for F-35 metrology data and contributing to next-generation LLM architectures for restricted programs — to leading structural design on liquid bi-propellant rockets at TREL and redesigning unsprung assemblies for Longhorn Racing Electric.
Outside of engineering, I serve as COO of Cascade, an agentic AI research platform for university life sciences labs, and mentor the next generation of robotics engineers through FRC Team 6800 Valor.
I'm drawn to problems where the physics is hard, the tolerances are tight, and the stakes are real.

bullets: Developed a Python multivariate regression model on SMR laser tracker & laser gauge metrology data to estimate F-35 fixture loading vectors — targeting elimination of an NC condition affecting ~33% of units (2–10 hrs unplanned rework each).
Automated QA workflows using Power Automate, reducing response times by 21.1% within three weeks.
Contributed to architecture of a private LLM for F-35, B-2, E-2D, and restricted programs; defined prompt hierarchy and test protocols.
Built a Power BI manager-suite dashboard with ML to fully automate a manual reporting process, saving ~312 hours annually.
Designed 3D-printed production aids with precision depth gauges, reducing F-35B tool validation time by 25%.
Root cause analysis on sealant disbond conditions revealed $800K+ annual rework cost; new procedures cut unit rework by 10–20 hours.
Developed Tableau dashboards for CMM Romer Arm metrology trends and implemented new planning changes.
Directed design of a 36" smokeless fire pit — solving airflow and heat distribution challenges with welded stainless steel construction for high-rate production.
Developed fully parametric SOLIDWORKS CAD models with bending and die design for flexible iteration.

academicProjects.label: Entrepreneurship — Active Venture
academicProjects.name: Cascade
academicProjects.desc: COO of a four-person startup building an agentic AI-powered research workflow platform for university medical and life sciences researchers. Architecting go-to-market strategy and operational infrastructure from zero to first customer.

academicProjects.label: ME 366J — Senior Design
academicProjects.name: Rotary Friction Test Bed
academicProjects.desc: Designing a tribometer-style apparatus with faculty advisors Dr. Madhavan and Dr. Khare. Full mechanical design, pneumatic actuator integration, and tribology data acquisition for friction characterization at variable loading conditions.

academicProjects.label: ME 144L — In Progress
academicProjects.name: DSC Final Project
academicProjects.desc: Dynamic Systems & Controls capstone project — details to be added upon completion.
